member.name: Tom Morrison
member.synopsis: Tom Morrison is the Liberal Democrat MP for Cheadle , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024. He currently undertakes the role of Liberal Democrat Deputy Chief Whip.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Menopause: Advice and Guidance Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment he has made of the impact of Advice and Guidance pathways on access to specialist care for women experiencing menopause-related health conditions.
writtenQuestions.answerText: No specific formal assessment has been made. Advice and Guidance is designed to support quicker, clearer clinical decision making, by enabling general practitioners (GPs) and specialists to discuss and agree the most appropriate next steps for a patient, ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Water Charges
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what assessment he has made of the potential impact of water companies’ environmental and service performance on water bills.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Ofwat’s outcome delivery incentives (ODIs) ensure that, where performance commitments are not met, companies must reimburse customers through lower water bills (known as ‘underperformance payments’) in the next financial year. As a result, for the draft d...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Hormone Replacement Therapy: Side Effects
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what guidance his Department has issued to NHS trusts and Integrated Care Boards on ensuring timely access to specialist care and treatment for women experiencing complications associated with horm
writtenQuestions.answerText: The National Institute for Health and Care Excellence’s (NICE) guideline on the identification and management of menopause includes guidance for healthcare professionals on the prescribing of hormone replacement therapy (HRT). The guideline includes guida...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Police: Workplace Pensions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what estimate her Department has made of the annual cost of allowing all surviving spouses, civil partners and partners receiving benefits under the Police Pension Scheme 1987 to retain those benefits
writtenQuestions.answerText: The most recent actuarial valuation of the police pension schemes in England and Wales as at 31 March 2020, shows that there are approximately 27,000 dependants in receipt of a pension. This figure includes dependants other than surviving partners and is ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Gaza: Israel
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what diplomatic steps she is taking to support the ceasefire in the Gaza war.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon Member to the statement provided to the House by the Foreign Secretary on 9 June, and her answers on this topic in the last session of departmental oral questions on 16 June.
